reflexion_entries_to_archive: S119-P1 #verbatim-move-plus-frozen-reference-differential: for a hot-loop extraction, transcribe the pre-refactor code INTO the test as a frozen reference and assert per-tick hash equality across forced-state scenarios — it catches transcription drift a two-run replay gate cannot (same bug on both runs), and it empirically re-verifies every deliberated substitution (per-frame alive-set) for free. PRIME-AUDIT R2 also killed an R1 events-seam design by tracing the CLIENT path: the edge watchers were shared, not host-only — trace the OTHER role's path before moving 'host' code.

S119-P2 #instrumented-twin-plus-throw-audit: when instrumenting a hot path, write the DEV branch as an exact call-twin of the prod line and prove parity by bundle-hash byte-identity; then audit what a mid-instrumentation THROW would skip (the send-gate stamp) — measurement code must be best-effort (try/catch) so the probe can never break the thing it measures.

S119-P3 #fix-the-warn-at-its-root-not-the-scanner: the every-boot ACTIVE-PLAN WARN was neither hook-bug nor stray files — archived plan BODIES still said STATUS: IN-PROGRESS; correcting the two stale lines (not touching the hook, not moving files) silenced it with the hook's own predicate as the verifier. Check what a scanner actually greps before assuming it misfires.
